name: Gorilla
summary: Gorillas are huge gentle apes that live in African forests. They eat plants, travel in family troops led by a silverback, and are among our closest living...
metaTitle: Gorilla Facts | Habitat, Diet, Troops & Photos
metaDescription: Discover gorilla facts with photos, forest habitat, plant diet, infant life, and conservation. Learn where gorillas live, what they eat, and how troops behave.
searchIntents: how do gorillas live in groups

lifespan.captivity: Up to 50 years in human care

speed: Up to 25 mph in short bursts on ground
funFacts: Gorillas share about 98 percent of their DNA with humans.
A dominant male gorilla is called a silverback because of the gray hair on his back.
Gorillas build a new leafy nest to sleep in almost every night.
Adult gorillas eat dozens of pounds of vegetation each day.
Gorillas usually beat their chests to warn rivals, not to start a fight.
Infants learn by watching mothers and other troop members.

faq.question: What is a gorilla?
faq.answer: A gorilla is a large ape that lives in African forests. Gorillas are plant-eaters and live in family groups called troops.

faq.question: Where do gorillas live?
faq.answer: Western lowland gorillas live in tropical forests and swampy lowlands in central and western Africa.

faq.question: What do gorillas eat?
faq.answer: Gorillas are mostly herbivores. They eat leaves, stems, fruit, and other forest plants.

faq.question: Are gorillas dangerous?
faq.answer: Gorillas are usually calm, but a silverback will defend his troop if he feels threatened. Wild gorillas should never be approached.

faq.question: Are gorillas endangered?
faq.answer: Yes. Western gorillas are listed as Critically Endangered because of habitat loss, disease, and hunting.

faq.question: How do gorillas live together?
faq.answer: Gorillas live in troops led by one dominant silverback male, along with females and young gorillas.

faq.question: How are gorillas related to humans?
faq.answer: Gorillas are great apes and are closely related to humans and chimpanzees.
